text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 30 March 2024 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Liam Carter, DOB: 22-07-1984, Hospital No: U817452 NHS No. 9384756102  
24 Brookside Close, London, N7 8QP 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Epilepsy with prior head injury history/low mood now improving with structured routine/vitamin D insufficiency on supplementation/smoker reducing intake. 

Medication: 
- Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily.  
- Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
- Vitamin D3 1000 IU once daily.  
- Omeprazole 20 mg once daily.  

I reviewed Liam Carter in clinic today. He attended alone and brought a detailed seizure diary and phone-based medication reminder logs. He reports good sleep hygiene, regular meals, and walking 20–30 minutes most days. He has used phone alarms and a weekly pill organiser consistently, which he feels has markedly improved his adherence and confidence. He notes better concentration and fewer morning headaches. On brief neurological examination today, speech was clear, gait steady, and there were no focal deficits elicited. Mood appeared brighter and he engaged well with the consultation. 

Seizures: He withdrew from Clobazam on 28-Dec. At that time, he had 4 seizures, one precipitated by illness. He has remained stable without seizures since. This improvement aligns with his consistent seizure diary and reminders, which have supported reliable dosing of his regular antiseizure medication. He has not required rescue medication and there have been no injuries or emergency attendances. He continues to avoid known precipitants such as missed doses and sleep deprivation. 

Adherence and safety: Phone reminders are set for 08:00 and 20:00 with a back-up alarm at +30 minutes. He has shared the plan with his partner, who cross-checks the weekly pill organiser on Sundays. He is aware of DVLA guidance and reports no episodes of loss of awareness since late December. He maintains hydration and limits caffeine to one cup daily. 

Plan: 
- Continue Levetiracetam and Lamotrigine at current doses; no changes today given sustained stability and clear improvement.  
- Continue seizure diary and digital reminders; bring logs to next review.  
- Routine bloods (U&E, LFTs, FBC, vitamin D) via practice in the next 4–6 weeks to support ongoing monitoring.  
- Provide patient with written information on missed-dose management and sick-day rules.  
- Follow-up in neurology clinic in 6 months, sooner if recurrence of events or adverse effects.  

Overall, his condition is improving with excellent adherence strategies and stable seizure control since late December. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: 1) Identify the clinic date: 30 March 2024. 2) Identify the last seizure events and their timing: 'He withdrew from Clobazam on 28-Dec. At that time, he had 4 seizures, one precipitated by illness.' 3) Identify the period since those seizures: 'He has remained stable without seizures since.' 4) Calculate the time from 28-Dec to 30-Mar: approximately 3 months (28-Dec to 28-Jan = 1 month; 28-Jan to 28-Feb = 1 month; 28-Feb to 28-Mar = 1 month; plus ~2 days). 5) Total seizures within this ~3-month interval = 4, all occurring at the start of the interval with none thereafter. 6) Therefore, the current frequency over the recent 3-month window is 4 per 3 months.
